Yeah. What's your kind of like longer-term plan? Because you've obviously gotten involved in Clearly
now and like &gt;&gt; Yeah. &gt;&gt; what's the plan? &gt;&gt; Honestly, I I've been thinking about
that a lot and I feel like I've changed my mind and like how I've wanted to kind of go about it for

have happened recently where it's different now, right? Like I think like agencies that are lean,
that have good margins, are looked at now kind of like how SaaS companies were looked at 5 years
ago. Um so I think like there's going to be a lot of agencies you'll see in the next year or so that
get really good multiples and are able to exit for for a good chunk of cash, which is great. Um but
to me like I I see agencies as a cash flow vehicle. So it's about, you know, maintaining really good
margins, being like kind of removed from the business so you're you know, you have like that great
freedom of lifestyle, but you have great margins, and you just take that and invest it into like
more stable investments, and you just do that for a long period of time. Um and and like the reason
I've kind of come to that conclusion is I just see like one of a one of our clients winning enough
probably has the lifestyle that like I like I would like to have when I am, you know, 40, 50 years
old. And and like for him it's like you know, he's he has a great work-life balance. He, you know,
is able to to sit with his family and have as many vacations. He has a a house in many areas. He has
all the super cars he could dream up. Like he has the lifestyle that I really want. And to the the
best advice that he gave me was this like, "Dude, like you shouldn't try to figure out how to 10x
your agency. You you should figure out how to get it to a really solid spot, have great team in
place, great margins in place, be really removed from the business, and just take the money,
consistently cash flow, and consistently invest into long-term assets like the S&amp;P and real
estate, and just worry about doing this for 20 years, and you will be in a very great space, and
you'll have a great quality of life." And I and I think that's that's the move. Cuz I think what I
see is like a lot of people will try to 10x their agency, and they're and and and I've been in that
spot before, and your margin just gets absolutely crushed, you know, when you try to 10x it. And
same thing too, like a lot of people they chase like the super high revenue number, and they never
keep in mind like their net margins. And it's like, you know, like if you look at some of the
largest lead gen agencies that are in the space right now, uh like the the ones that are I don't
want to name names and and how I find these details and stuff, but like the the very top ones, the
ones that are doing like 20, 30 million dollars a year, um their founders, their the CEOs, they're
taking less than a lot of people that are doing like 6 to 10 million dollars a year, right? And so
that to me is like, why would I want to be at like 30 million dollars in revenue, have such a
massive team, massive organization, and personally take home as much as someone that is 3x smaller
than me. Like that to me just doesn't make sense. It's like 3x the or you know, like 5 to 10x the
responsibility but making the same as someone that is 3x smaller. And so that that's kind of how I I
think about it. Like I I yeah, I I don't think I want a 10x agency. I just want to have consistent,
stable growth and just play the long game. And I think that's that's the thing. I think a lot of
people are just trying to chase like the shiny object, trying to scale super quick, and then it just
never never pays out of their favor. So that's kind of like the opposite of what I want to do. Um
And I think it took me a while to kind of get to that idea cuz I at one point I was like, "Dude, I

year." And and not to say that I don't think I can get there. I think I will get there over time,
